HO-Azure :: attack-path-focused Azure recon
context :: tenant=auto subscription=auto output=table

[privesc] Triage likely privilege-escalation and workload identity abuse paths.

│ priority │ starting foothold                  │ path type                       │ target                               │
├──────────┼────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────┤
│ high     │ azurefox-lab-sp (current foothold) │ current foothold direct control │ current foothold (service principal) │
╰──────────┴────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ note                                                                                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Preferred foothold: current foothold azurefox-lab-sp (ServicePrincipal).                    │
│ It already has direct high-impact RBAC on visible scope.                                    │
│ Current foothold 'azurefox-lab-sp' already holds high-impact RBAC (Owner) on visible scope. │
│                                                                                             │
│ HO-Azure does not prove which exact abuse action is the best next step from this row alone. │
│ Next review: rbac assignment evidence and scope.                                            │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

╭──────────┬────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────╮
│ priority │ starting foothold                  │ path type                        │ target                                │
├──────────┼────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────────────────┤
│ medium   │ azurefox-lab-sp (current foothold) │ ingress-backed workload identity │ managed identity ua-app via vm-web-01 │
╰──────────┴────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────────────────╯
╭──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ note                                                                                 │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Public workload 'vm-web-01' carries identity 'ua-app' with high-impact RBAC (Owner). │
│                                                                                      │
│ HO-Azure does not prove control of the workload or successful token use from it.     │
│ Next review: managed-identities workload-to-identity anchor.                         │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Takeaway: 2 privilege-escalation paths surfaced; 1 current-identity-rooted, 1 visible-only lead, 1 high, 1 medium.
